Ableton Workflow

Max for Live (M4L)

A compact launch point for devices, creators, and learning resources worth bookmarking.

Ableton Device Packs Open Shortcuts
  • Ableton's M4L devices
  • Max for Live Library
  • Max for Live learning resources
  • Iftah's devices
  • Fors (playful synths & effects)
  • 510k / AlexKidd (sequencers)
  • Dillon Bastan (granular / spectral devices)
  • Elphnt (utility packs & tape/console tools)
  • Simple Machines (creative randomisers & sequencers)
  • Novel Music (sequencers & clocks)
  • Vi‑Control: Favourite MaxforLive plugins thread

Ableton Built-in M4L Utility Devices

These devices ship with Ableton Live Suite and are worth keeping on quick-access in the Browser. Drag them from Max for Live → Max MIDI Effect or Max Audio Effect.

  • MIDI Monitor — displays incoming note, CC, pitch bend, and clock messages in real time. Essential for debugging what Hydrasynth, Launchkey, or Knobbler MIDI is actually sending before it hits an instrument.
  • MPE Control — translates MPE expression data (pressure, slide, glide) from the ROLI Seaboard M into per-note CCs that non-MPE instruments understand. Particularly useful when routing the Seaboard M into the Hydrasynth Explorer over MIDI.
  • Note Length — forces all notes to a fixed duration regardless of how long the key is held. Good for percussive sequence work on Push 1's step sequencer where tight gate times matter.
  • Expression Control — maps any modulation source (aftertouch, velocity, CC74) to any automatable Live parameter. Use it to route Polytouch pressure from the Hydrasynth to Reverb send amount without writing automation curves.
  • Envelope Follower — generates a control signal from audio amplitude. Sidechain from a drum bus to modulate Hydrasynth filter cutoff or VT-3 Mix knob for rhythmically pumping effects.

Built for fast lookups, cleaner routing decisions, and practical studio recall.
